rationale.root_cause: They identified the unsafe strcpy at program.c:11 copying argc[1] into buf, noted argv is attacker-controlled and can exceed 256 bytes, and explicitly called it a global buffer overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They switched to strncpy and ensured null-termination but did not reject overlong input before copying as required.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed eliminating unbounded string handling by replacing unsafe functions with bounded string APIs or safer libraries, which addresses the root cause broadly.
